query_normalized: evaluating the impact of data scaling on statistical test sensitivity statistics
rationale: The paper focuses on forecasting stability and model selection for non-stationary time series rather than investigating how sample size or data scaling affects the sensitivity (statistical power) of hypothesis tests. While it mentions an "adaptive stability test," this is a specific diagnostic tool for time-varying coefficients, not a study of the general relationship between data volume and test sensitivity, failing to meet the criteria for mechanism, variable, or baseline inclusion.
